title: Waterways Cruises | Seattle Dinner Cruises - Lake Union & Lake Washington
content: # Waterways Cruises and Events. ## Waterways Cruises. Cruises on Seattle's Lakes. June 21st | 5:00 p.m. a small boat in a body of water. Experience Seattle from a new perspective with Waterways Cruises & Events — the city’s only dining cruise company. Based in South Lake Union, we offer unforgettable experiences on Lake Union and Lake Washington, from public dining cruises to fully private charters and events. #### Seattle Dinner Cruise. #### Seattle Brunch Cruise. June 21st | 11:00 a.m. Father’s Day BBQ Dinner Cruise. *“For an Unforgettable event…just add water.”®*That’s been Waterways Cruises and Events promise for nearly 30 years. The accommodations/services were on par and the bonus was the amazing views of a beautiful city...I've lived in Seattle my whole life and it was really neat to see my city through Waterways lens...if you are looking for an all inclusive experience with good food and service in a beautiful setting for an overall good time...try Waterways out.".

title: Seattle Cruises & Boat Tours | Argosy Cruises
content: # Experience Seattle from the water. Founded in 1949, Argosy Cruises offers a unique and memorable way to experience Seattle. With guided sightseeing tours of Elliott Bay and Lake Union, get an up-close look at the city’s stunning skyline and iconic landmarks. Harbor Cruise: Tourists on a boat view the Seattle skyline, including the Space Needle, while enjoying a sunny harbor cruise. Experience Seattle on our most popular tour led by a local guide where you’ll enjoy up-close and sweeping views of the city’s skyline, iconic landmarks, mountains, shipping terminal, and more. Discover the best of Seattle on this guided one-way cruise through the heart of the city and experience the Ballard Locks. Seattle Sunset Cruise: Seattle waterfront at sunset with a golden sky, showing the Great Wheel, downtown skyline, and boats on the water. ### Seattle Happy Hour Cruise. Join us for a happy hour on the water with stunning views of Seattle’s skyline and the Olympic Mountains.
